ddls:
CREATE TABLE "films" (
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each film */,
  "imdb_id" TEXT /* IMDB identifier for the film */,
  "original_title" TEXT /* Original title of the film */,
  "director" TEXT /* Director of the film */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the film */,
  "release_year" INTEGER /* Year the film was released */,
  "genres" TEXT /* Genres associated with the film (comma-separated) */,
  "popularity" REAL /* Popularity score of the film */,
  "production_company" TEXT /* Name of the production company */,
  "country_of_origin" TEXT /* Country where the film was produced */,
  "runtime_minutes" INTEGER /* Duration of the film in minutes */,
  "tagline" TEXT /* Tagline or catchphrase of the film */,
  "synopsis" TEXT /* Brief summary or plot of the film */,
  "rating" REAL /* Average rating of the film (e.g., from 1 to 10) */,
  "num_votes" INTEGER /* Number of votes or ratings received */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("film_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_films_director FOREIGN KEY ("director") REFERENCES directors ("name")
)

CREATE TABLE "financials" (
  "financial_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each financial record */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film associated with financial data */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the film */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Revenue generated by the film */,
  "budget_adj" REAL /* Adjusted budget for inflation */,
  "revenue_adj" REAL /* Adjusted revenue for inflation */,
  "domestic_revenue" REAL /* Revenue generated within the country of origin */,
  "international_revenue" REAL /* Revenue generated outside the country of origin */,
  "marketing_budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for marketing and promotion */,
  "profit" REAL /* Calculated profit (revenue - budget) */,
  "roi" REAL /* Return on Investment (ROI) percentage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("financial_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_financials_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "directors" (
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each director */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the director */,
  "birth_year" INTEGER /* Birth year of the director */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the director */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the director */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the director */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Brief biography of the director */,
  "filmography_count" INTEGER /* Number of films directed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("director_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the genre */,
  "subgenres" TEXT /* Subgenres associated with the genre (comma-separated) */,
  "popularity_score" REAL /* Popularity score of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "film_genres" (
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film ID */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the genre ID */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("film_id", "genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, production staff) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., production, marketing, finance) */,
  "access_level" INTEGER /* Access level of the user (e.g., 1 for basic, 2 for intermediate, 3 for admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user accessing the data */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of the access event */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the data */,
  "session_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the user's session in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)
